turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Good afternoon. I'm calling to check whether United Health Care TPA is accepted at your clinic for a planned procedure.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Good afternoon! Yes, United Health Care TPA is on our approved list. What procedure are you looking at?

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: It's a laparoscopic cholecystectomy — gallbladder removal. My name is Geeta Venkataraman.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Ms. Venkataraman, that procedure is covered under most TPA plans. You'll need a pre-authorization letter from your insurer before admission. Our TPA coordinator will help initiate that on your behalf if you come in for a pre-admission consultation.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: How far in advance do I need to do that?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: We recommend at least 3 to 5 working days before your surgery date. It gives enough time for back-and-forth with the insurance company if any documents are needed.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Understood. And what documents do I bring for the consultation?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Bring your insurance card, employee ID if it's a group policy, the surgeon's referral, and any relevant diagnostic reports like ultrasound results. Our TPA desk is open weekdays 9 AM to 5 PM.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Happy to help, Ms. Venkataraman. Wishing you a smooth procedure!
